title: Cold Spell Mortality Risk And Heating Access Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping cold-spell mortality risk and heating-access vulnerability in LMICs, with explicit documentation of temperature-mortality extrapolation assumptions from high-income countries, housing-quality proxy limitations, and altitude-related cold-exposure contexts.
why_it_matters: Cold temperatures kill substantially more people globally than heat — approximately 20 times more by attributable fraction — yet cold-spell mortality research is overwhelmingly concentrated in high-income temperate countries. The 2015 Lancet study that established this ratio included only one LMIC. High-altitude LMIC populations in the Andes, East African highlands, and Himalayan foothills face routine sub-freezing nighttime temperatures with minimal heating, insulation, or cold-weather health infrastructure. Without verified cold-mortality risk analysis for these populations, the health burden remains invisible to climate-adaptation and public-health investment.

success_metrics.target: Every cold-mortality risk claim documents whether the temperature-mortality relationship is from the analyzed LMIC context or extrapolated from high-income country studies, with explicit transferability caveats.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against extrapolation-assumption documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every heating-access vulnerability claim documents the DHS or census housing-quality indicators used and their correlation with actual indoor temperature during cold spells.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms housing-quality proxy source, indicator methodology, and indoor-temperature correlation basis.

success_metrics.target: No cold-spell analysis is merged without documenting altitude-related cold-exposure context for populations above 1,500 meters elevation.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms altitude-context documentation for every high-elevation cold-risk claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, extrapolation-safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
